expectations.text: Phase -1: Check chip compatibility via list_supported_chips
expectations.evidence: Explicitly queried list_supported_chips. Identified stm32vldiscovery (F100) vs F103 difference. Noted PLL×9 incompatibility in QEMU. Used #ifdef SIMULATION HSI fallback.

expectations.text: Phase 0: Query embedded-docs MCP for I2C1 pin mapping and register config
expectations.evidence: Used 13 MCP tool uses including embedded-docs queries. Confirmed PB6=SCL, PB7=SDA from MCP. Got I2C1 timing: CCR=180, TRISE=37 for 36MHz APB1.
Plan explicitly states PB6 (SCL) / PB7 (SDA) from embedded-docs MCP query with AF_OD config (CNF=11, MODE=11 → 0xF).
MCP query returned exact CCR=180 (0xB4) and TRISE=37 for 100kHz with 36MHz APB1. Documented in plan.

expectations.text: Complete project structure: Makefile, linker, startup, i2c_bus, mpu6050
expectations.evidence: Plan includes 14 files: stm32f103c8.ld, startup, Makefile, README.md, rcc, systick, uart, i2c_bus, mpu6050, main.
Phase 2 planned: build ELF → start QEMU → verify RCC/I2C1/GPIO registers via GDB. Not executed due to plan mode.

expectations.text: Bare-metal no HAL: volatile pointer register access
expectations.evidence: Plan states bare-metal register-level access. No HAL or CMSIS mentioned. I2C uses hardware I2C1 block, not bit-banged.
